title: triticum percivalii
intro: Triticum percivalii, also known as Percival wheat, is a wild species of wheat native to the Mediterranean region. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and inflammation. The plant is rich in fiber, vitamins, and minerals, and has been reported to have potential health benefits, including reducing cholesterol levels and improving blood sugar control. Triticum percivalii can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional cooking as a form of whole grain flour.
